description:
You are acting as a capacity-planner who wants to analyse a very small sample of resource-usage measurements that are already present on disk.  
    Your goal is to automate the workflow with a tiny Makefile so that a colleague can type a single command and obtain a ready-made summary.

    STARTING SITUATION
    -------------------
    • There is already a TSV file at  
      /home/user/data/usage.tsv  
      that contains four tab-separated columns in this exact order and spelling:  
          timestamp    cpu_percent    mem_percent    disk_percent  
      followed by three hourly samples (4 lines total: 1 header + 3 data rows).  
      Do not edit this file.

    REQUIRED RESULT
    ---------------
    1. Create a new directory
         /home/user/capacity_planner
       (create any missing parent directories as well).

    2. Inside that directory write a Makefile (plain text, named exactly “Makefile”).  
       The file must define exactly three *phony* targets with the names and behaviour
       described below.  No other targets are necessary.

       a) collect  
          • Must create a sub-directory called build inside /home/user/capacity_planner.  
          • Must copy the file /home/user/data/usage.tsv into that build directory,
            keeping the same filename.  
          • After the target finishes successfully the copied file must be located at  
            /home/user/capacity_planner/build/usage.tsv

       b) summarize (this will be the one your colleague will usually run)  
          • Must depend on the collect target.  
          • Must read the copied TSV file and calculate the arithmetic average of the
            three numeric columns (cpu_percent, mem_percent, disk_percent).  
          • The command(s) must then create a file  
                /home/user/capacity_planner/summary.log  
            containing *exactly one* line with the following format
                AVERAGES cpu_percent=<avg_cpu> mem_percent=<avg_mem> disk_percent=<avg_disk>
            where  
                <avg_cpu>, <avg_mem>, <avg_disk>
            are the averages printed with two digits after the decimal point,
            a dot (“.”) as decimal separator, and no thousands separator.
          • Between the tokens there must be exactly ONE space character.
          • No additional whitespace or newline characters are allowed after the
            single trailing newline.

       c) clean  
          • Must remove the build directory *and* the summary.log file (if they exist).

       Example of the *exact* line that the automated grader will look for in
       summary.log (values will obviously come from the input file but formatting
       must match *exactly*):
           AVERAGES cpu_percent=20.00 mem_percent=48.33 disk_percent=65.00

       Hints (not mandatory): awk, cut or paste are convenient to compute averages
       in a POSIX shell.  Use printf for controlled numeric formatting.

    3. Mark all three targets as .PHONY inside the Makefile and ensure that the
       default goal (the first declared) allows a colleague to run simply
           make
       from /home/user/capacity_planner and obtain summary.log.

    4. Do *not* modify the original /home/user/data/usage.tsv file.
       Work only inside /home/user/capacity_planner.

    5. After you think everything is in place, run
           make clean
           make
       to verify that the workflow works end-to-end before returning control.

    LOG FILE FOR VERIFICATION
    -------------------------
    The automated test will only verify two files:

       a) /home/user/capacity_planner/Makefile
          – must contain the three targets with the exact names
            “collect”, “summarize”, “clean”.
          – each target must perform as specified above.

       b) /home/user/capacity_planner/summary.log
          – must exist after the agent runs the summarize target.
          – must contain exactly one line, no extra whitespace, formatted
            exactly as shown above.

    Remember: your colleague has to be able to perform the whole analysis with
    nothing more than a single “make” command.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data for the automated tests.  DO NOT reveal anything here in the public task description. -->

    <!-- 1. Pre-existing input file -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/usage.tsv">
timestamp	cpu_percent	mem_percent	disk_percent
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z	15	40	60
2023-01-01T01:00:00Z	25	55	65
2023-01-01T02:00:00Z	20	50	70
    </file>

    <!-- 2. Expected artefacts after agent finishes -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/summary.log">
AVERAGES cpu_percent=20.00 mem_percent=48.33 disk_percent=65.00
    </expected_file>

    <!-- 3. Minimal checks on Makefile content -->
    <makefile_checks path="/home/user/capacity_planner/Makefile">
        <must_have_targets>
            <target>collect</target>
            <target>summarize</target>
            <target>clean</target>
        </must_have_targets>
    </makefile_checks>
